title: Session Stream
statement: AI work unfolds as a continuous workspace rather than a series of closed sessions.
summary: Designed a mobile-first navigation model in which documents, conversations, and active contexts are traversed through continuous vertical movement instead of repeated session switching.

detail.problem: Conventional AI interfaces split work into separate sessions, forcing users to repeatedly enter, exit, and reconstruct context across isolated views.
detail.designMove: Recast sessions as positions inside a single spatial stream, so movement across tasks happens through scrolling rather than through container changes.
detail.outcome: Shifts conversation from session management to spatial navigation, making context easier to retain, revisit, and work across.

media.alt: Mobile interface showing continuous scrolling between AI sessions and documents.

impact: Turns multi-session interaction into a spatial navigation model.

title: Meta Control
statement: System behavior is edited in context through language instead of hidden control panels.
summary: Designed an interaction layer where users can change document type, access state, and behavioral rules directly from within the workspace using natural language.

detail.problem: In most systems, configuration is pushed into secondary panels and technical metadata flows, separating operational control from the work itself.
detail.designMove: Placed behavioral control inside the primary interface by letting users issue structural changes in language, with explicit application states that keep the system legible.
detail.outcome: Turns configuration into a first-class interaction, allowing system behavior to be modified where the work is actually happening.

media.alt: User changing document type and metadata through natural language commands.

impact: Makes system-level behavior editable through interaction instead of hidden configuration.

title: Header System
statement: Documents operate as active system objects rather than passive files.
summary: Designed a document model where structured headers define execution logic, permissions, and system behavior, making control state readable and editable as part of the document itself.

detail.problem: Most software separates content from behavior, leaving users to navigate between the document they are working on and the hidden structures that determine how it behaves.
detail.designMove: Promoted the document header into an explicit control surface, so the governing logic of a document can be inspected, modified, and applied in place.
detail.outcome: Repositions documents as programmable interface units whose structure is directly usable rather than concealed in the system layer.

media.alt: Document header being modified and applied as a control layer.

impact: Turns documents into active components of system behavior.

title: Ghostwriting
statement: AI assistance appears inside the draft instead of in a separate conversational channel.
summary: Designed an in-place co-writing mode where generated output is inserted directly into the document, keeping composition and assistance inside the same working surface.

detail.problem: Typical AI writing tools split drafting and assistance across two interfaces, which breaks writing flow and turns revision into a context-switching task.
detail.designMove: Collapsed the editor and the assistant into one surface, so generation happens exactly where the text is being written and revised.
detail.outcome: Produces a co-writing model in which AI behaves as part of the drafting environment rather than as an external sidecar.

media.alt: AI writing directly inside a text document alongside user input.

impact: Integrates AI into the writing surface instead of separating it.
